title: SEELE and the Angels
wikitext:
[]: SEELE and the Angels             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Feb 13, 2008 6:40 pm OK. We all know that Seele, at the beginning of the series was represented as the group of old men around a table, but changes later in the series to a circle of monoliths. But there was no explanation for this change. I was reading in the wiki page that Analyzed Episode 21 the monoliths could represent the souls of the Angels returning to Adam (in Kaworu's conversation with Seele). If the monoliths represent the souls of the Angels, or are the souls of the angels, then what relation does that have to the old men of Seele? (If my question doesn't sound clear, read the wiki page. Thank you)

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Wed Feb 13, 2008 7:30 pm Good question, but why would the old men of Seele want to initiate Third Impact in a way that would destroy the Angels...?...if they are the Angels...? Or maybe since the old men of Seele come from different parts of the world, perhaps they do represent the souls of the destroyed Angels...who come from the same place as they did. Yeah, I'm thinking they are the Angels...when I think about it...it's plausible.

[]: Re: SEELE and the Angels             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Feb 13, 2008 8:12 pm Sachiel_13 wrote: OK. We all know that Seele, at the beginning of the series was represented as the group of old men around a table, but changes later in the series to a circle of monoliths. But there was no explanation for this change.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Feb 15, 2008 8:28 pm At least as I last proposed it, the theory holds that two different things are happening in that scene: Kaworu's final conversation with the Old Men (presumably the committee, as they are the only ones who speak) prior to leaving for Japan is being "reenacted". However, at that actual moment in time (at the lake), it's only Kaworu "talking to himself" and repeating what he had said previously. (Why? Uh... It's Kaworu. He has some serious emotional problems.) Aside from being a part of the "reenactment" and "not actually there", the Seele monoliths are serving another purpose, and that is as representations of the dead Angel's souls. The "Ghosts of the Lake" theory is a "follow-up" to the one about "what Armisael was really doing in #23" -- i.e., "I'm ronery, so I'm going to collect the souls of my dead sibs and try to resurrect them using Eva-00". After Armisael and Eva-00 are destroyed, the souls become "residents" of the Tokyo-3 lake, and, in the #24' scene, have gathered around Kaworu, each one represented by a separate monolith . This is the "real" reason why fifteen monoliths are shown, as opposed to the twelve we see in every other scene with Seele. It's also worth noting that the word "Seele" means "soul", and that gravemarkers in NGE are in the shape of monoliths. When the monoliths vanish, the souls are presumed to have been absorbed by Kaworu (insert joke about vagina palms here) for employment in one of the potential outcomes of the Terminal Dogma venture. More can be found in this epic thread .

[]:             Quote Post by RandomStuff » Sun Feb 17, 2008 7:17 pm I had always assumed that the committee used the monoliths after a certain point for some kind of security reason. I never gave thought to how many there were.

[]:             Quote Post by evilhead008 » Mon Feb 18, 2008 9:26 pm That's.... a really great idea... The convinient diagram by Reichu helps a lot, too. OH YEAH WHILE WE'RE ON THIS TOPIC, WE'VE SETTLED THE ISRAFEL DEBATE! IF THIS THEORY IS CORRECT, ISRAFEL MUST HAVE 2 TWIN SOULS, OR ELSE WE HAVE AN EXTRA MONOLITH! If Kaworu is Adam, is he/she talking to his/her dead children, or siblings? This kind of seems like the "MP Evas contain Angel Souls" thing... not really impossible, but improbably (at least to me).[/b]

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Feb 18, 2008 11:23 pm No. This Seele-Angel relationship theory appears to have little substance. How we went from "there's an animation error and there are 15 Seele monoliths" to "they must represent Angel souls" evades me. Further, we're not entirely sure how Israfel's soul works, but the problem is....this doesn't explain how the "Iruel" cells all could have one souls and not millions of souls. Would we then have millions of Seele monoliths? (creating a gravity well and collapsing us into a new star...Jupiter beware...what, no one saw 2010?! ) anyway, just as Iruel could have one "soul", Israfel really seems to have one "soul" divided into two bodies at times. Nonetheless, jumping to the assumption that "Israfel must count twice in order to add up to 15" is just trying to fit the facts into the theory intstead of the other way around. I'm sorry but this doesn't really seem to have any merit to me. My official view is: "Because we later see 12 Seele Monoliths in EoE, the 15 Seele monoliths in episode 24 are simply an animation error, plain and simple" and "while this might be telepathic communication, it's probably Kaworu "replaying" his memories...as a clunky exposition device to explain the Adam-Kaworu connection to the audience".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Feb 18, 2008 11:40 pm V wrote: No.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Feb 19, 2008 12:03 am Would we then have millions of Seele monoliths? (creating a gravity well and collapsing us into a new star...Jupiter beware...what, no one saw 2010?! )

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Feb 19, 2008 12:12 am If we're gonna argue about Israfel's Soul(s), you might as well do it in the designated thread. http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=4119&start=0 And a split could be in hand for Iruel's soul(s). But for that I agree with Reichu. Iruel is one soul organized of millions of tiny nano-bodies.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Feb 19, 2008 12:26 am well I don't see why the "Iruel principle" isn't being applied to Israfel too

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Feb 19, 2008 4:55 am Israfel's antics with its core were one reason, as were two giant entities working together, as opposed to a swarm of microbes increasing crazily in number. The Room of Gaf is empty, so having millions of new souls shouldn't be possible at all. Instead of having one soul that splits, recombines and splits into two again, two separate ones inhabiting a single body makes sense. Especially with that episode's theme about synchronizing with each other.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Feb 19, 2008 8:02 am You need to define what a soul is in NGE before you start talking about if it can be split between two vessels or whatever else. If the soul in NGE is simply a wavepattern or some kind of field that stores information like DNA then there's no reason why it couldn't be replicated, and that cores could divide themselves like cells. If you accept the theory that Lilith's soul is split between Eva-00 and Rei, and if you accept the theory that Kyoko's soul was split between her Lilin body and Eva-02 after her contact experiment - then you shouldn't have any problem believing that Israfel and Ireul's souls can do the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Feb 19, 2008 8:31 am Zugzwang wrote: If you accept the theory that Lilith's soul is split between Eva-00 and Rei, and if you accept the theory that Kyoko's soul was split between her Lilin body and Eva-02 after her contact experiment - then you shouldn't have any problem believing that Israfel and Ireul's souls can do the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Feb 19, 2008 8:50 am Zugzwang wrote: You need to define what a soul is in NGE before you start talking about if it can be split between two vessels or whatever else. If the soul in NGE is simply a wavepattern or some kind of field that stores information like DNA then there's no reason why it couldn't be replicated, and that cores could divide themselves like cells.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Feb 19, 2008 10:37 am Doesn't JA guy say that it's just a matter of time until they can produce an ATF artificially? An ATF means a soul is involved somewhere, whether he meant an artificial soul or a soul embedded in a machine I dunno. You have to ask what the Guf is again, as it seems to have something to do with the Dirac Sea, and the Dirac Sea to do with the S2 theory, so it might just be their lack of understanding how to access the Dirac Sea or to create negative energy, or whatever it is, that holds the world back from creating souls in NGE. The thing about the cores merging together again - Misato explains that a two-point attack on both cores will make them join together again. I don't think I understand what you're getting at.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Feb 19, 2008 11:39 am That was just the general i-am-the-arrogant-smirkface-who-doesn't-really-know-anything type of thing. It's not like they knew anything really about Eva's, Ritsuko is perfectly right with her comments in ep 7

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Feb 19, 2008 12:32 pm Zugzwang wrote: Doesn't JA guy say that it's just a matter of time until they can produce an ATF artificially? An ATF means a soul is involved somewhere, whether he meant an artificial soul or a soul embedded in a machine I dunno.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Feb 19, 2008 1:52 pm Ornette wrote: ep07 wrote: RITSUKO: It doesn't matter what you say. The fact is that only Nerv's weapons have the ability to defeat the enemy. TOKITA: You mean the AT field? That's just a matter of time as well. Nerv's day in the sun will not last forever.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Feb 19, 2008 2:04 pm Yes the JA guy didn't know how AT Fields are REALLY made and didn't realize you need a soul to do it and probably the organic Eva bodies too. He didn't know what he was talking about.
